quests.giver: Hestu, southeast of Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower (northwest of New Serenne Stable)
quests.reward: Unlocks Hestu's pouch (weapon/bow/shield) expansions for Korok Seeds
quests.oneLine: Hestu is too scared of nearby 'trees' to do business — deal with them so he can expand your pouch.
quests.how: Find Hestu at Lindor's Brow; he is frightened of the surrounding trees, which are actually Evermean monsters. Defeat the Evermeans (fire works well, or chop them with a sharp weapon) to calm Hestu, after which he stays and trades Korok Seeds for inventory-slot upgrades. (If you finish a temple/Regional Phenomenon before meeting him, Hestu instead relocates to Lookout Landing.)

quests.name: The Heroines' Secret
quests.giver: Rotana, the Gerudo archaeologist, in the Gerudo Shelter beneath Gerudo Town
quests.reward: A mysterious orb (used in The Mysterious Eighth)
quests.oneLine: Photograph four hidden stelae about the seven Gerudo heroines for Rotana.
quests.how: Use the Purah Pad camera to photograph all four stelae in the Shelter: behind Rotana's room (clear the rubble first), by the stairs in the mural room, in the classroom (visit late evening/early morning, not during 11AM-3PM school hours), and in the jail (get yourself arrested by removing your top or wearing the Yiga Mask). Bring the pictures back to Rotana.

quests.name: The Mysterious Eighth
quests.giver: Rotana in the Gerudo Shelter, after The Heroines' Secret
quests.reward: A Diamond
quests.oneLine: Gather eight patterned orbs to unlock a hidden chamber proving an eighth Gerudo heroine existed.
quests.how: Collect the eight orbs (from The Heroines' Secret, Dalia's Game, Lost in the Dunes, plus ones hidden in dorm bunk beds and supply boxes around the Shelter/town), then use Ultrahand to set each orb on a statue's hands to open the secret room. Ultrahand the giant orb out to the North Gerudo Ruins, work through the Gerudo Sanctuary (beat the Gibdos and clear obstacles), read the stone tablet, and report back to Rotana.

quests.name: Dalia's Game
quests.giver: Dalia, near the main entrance inside Gerudo Town
quests.reward: An orb (one of the eight for The Mysterious Eighth)
quests.oneLine: Win Dalia's little hide-and-seek/guessing game to earn an orb.
quests.how: Talk to Dalia by the main gate and play her game to win, which hands you one of the patterned orbs needed for The Mysterious Eighth.

quests.name: The Missing Owner
quests.giver: Cara, the jewelry-shop worker (in the Gerudo Shelter, or out front of the Jewelry Shop once town is restored)
quests.reward: A Diamond (save it for Pride of the Gerudo)
quests.oneLine: Track down the missing jewelry-shop owner Isha, stranded out in the desert.
quests.how: Head west to Toruma Dunes and look for the plume of smoke; crouch (L) to stay undetected by the Molduga, then rescue Isha on the rock formation. Beat the Molduga (toss a Brightbloom Seed or bomb on the sand to make it surface, hit it with bombs/arrows, then melee its belly while it's stunned), then meet Isha and Cara back at the shop for your Diamond.

quests.name: Pride of the Gerudo
quests.giver: Isha at the Jewelry Shop in Gerudo Town, after The Missing Owner
quests.reward: Scimitar of the Seven and Daybreaker shield
quests.oneLine: Bring Isha rare materials so she can forge the legendary Gerudo blade and shield.
quests.how: Gather and give Isha four Diamonds, ten Flint, a Gerudo Scimitar, and a Gerudo Shield; she then crafts and hands you the Scimitar of the Seven and the Daybreaker.

quests.name: Treasure of the Gerudo Desert
quests.giver: Riju in the throne room, after completing the Riju of Gerudo Town main quest
quests.location: Gerudo Town / West Gerudo Underground Ruins (West Barrens)
quests.reward: Vah Naboris Divine Helm
quests.oneLine: Solve Riju's riddle about the desert's sword-pointing warrior statues to find buried treasure.
quests.how: Travel northwest to the West Barrens, find the large bone ruins with a cracked patch of ground, and blow it open with a Bomb Flower to drop into the West Gerudo Underground Ruins. Follow the direction the Warrior Statues' swords point, clear the boulders blocking the way, and open the chest at the end for the Vah Naboris Divine Helm.

quests.name: To the Ruins!
quests.giver: Pokki, at the Sand-Seal Rental Shop in Gerudo Town (after Riju of Gerudo Town)
quests.reward: A Silver Rupee (100 rupees)
quests.oneLine: Give Pokki a smooth sand-seal sleigh ride out to the East Gerudo Ruins.
quests.how: Rent a Sleigh Seal from the sand-seal stall (she insists on the sleigh, not a plain seal), pick up Pokki, and drive carefully east to the East Gerudo Ruins, avoiding monster camps and sinkholes; sharp swerves or damage send you back to start over. Arrive intact for the Silver Rupee.

quests.name: Lost in the Dunes
quests.giver: Benja at Kara Kara Bazaar
quests.location: Kara Kara Bazaar / Oasis Source cavern
quests.reward: A patterned orb (for The Mysterious Eighth)
quests.oneLine: Rescue the missing caravan merchant Ponthos from an underground cavern near the oasis.
quests.how: Drop into the Kara Kara Bazaar well and follow the water, or dive into the nearby sandpit, to reach the underground Oasis Source where Ponthos is trapped. Fuse a rock-breaking weapon (or use bombs) to smash the boulders blocking the tunnels, follow the water northeast back up to the bazaar, then talk to Benja and Ponthos to earn the orb.

quests.name: Decorate with Passion
quests.giver: Boraa at Kara Kara Bazaar
quests.reward: An Electric Keese Wing (decorative material)
quests.oneLine: Show Boraa a ruby-fused weapon so she can use the gem as a decoration.
quests.how: Fuse a Ruby onto any weapon (Fuse via the right d-pad ability) and show the ruby-tipped weapon to Boraa; she hands you an Electric Keese Wing in return.

quests.name: The Iceless Icehouse
quests.giver: Anche at the Northern Icehouse
quests.location: Northern Icehouse (Gerudo Desert, north of Gerudo Town)
quests.reward: A Purple Rupee (50 rupees); repeatable daily
quests.oneLine: Make a fresh block of ice for Anche's empty icehouse using elemental tricks.
quests.how: Freeze the puddle in the middle of the room (throw an Ice Fruit or White Chuchu Jelly into it, or hit it with a Sapphire-fused weapon) to form a big ice block, then shave it down with a single Fire Fruit / Ruby-fused weapon / torch to the right size. Use Ultrahand to set the ice into the storage beside Anche and talk to her for the reward.

quests.name: Gleeok Guts
quests.giver: Malena at the Gerudo Canyon Stable area / Gerudo Desert Gateway (south of the Gerudo Canyon Well)
quests.location: Gerudo Canyon / Gerudo Desert (Flame Gleeok near Spectacle Rock)
quests.reward: 300 rupees (a Gold Rupee)
quests.oneLine: Fetch Flame Gleeok guts for Malena to cure her ailing husband.
quests.how: Travel to the Flame Gleeok southwest of the Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower (near Spectacle Rock); shoot each of its three heads with arrows until it crashes down, then melee its body. Hand the Gleeok Guts to Malena for the 300-rupee reward.

quests.name: Cold-Endurance Contest!
quests.giver: Rahdo, at the summit of Mount Granajh in Gerudo Canyon (southeast of the Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower)
quests.reward: 100 rupees (the combined 50-rupee pot)
quests.oneLine: Outlast an old man through the freezing desert night with cold resistance.
quests.how: Pay Rahdo 50 rupees to start, then survive the cold longer than he does by eating a cold-resistance dish or fusing a Ruby (or Fire Fruit) to your weapon for warmth; do not use a campfire. Last until morning to win the pot.

quests.name: Heat-Endurance Contest!
quests.giver: Rahdo, at the summit of Mount Granajh in Gerudo Canyon, after Cold-Endurance Contest!
quests.reward: 300 rupees (a Gold Rupee, the combined 150-rupee pot)
quests.oneLine: Outlast the old man through the scorching desert day after stripping off your gear.
quests.how: Pay Rahdo 150 rupees to start; you must strip to your undergarments (no heat-resist armor allowed), so keep your body temperature down by fusing a Sapphire (or Ice Fruit) to your weapon/shield while standing the heat. Outlast him to win the pot.

quests.name: Piaffe, Packed Away
quests.giver: Piaffe, stuck behind crates inside Gerudo Canyon Stable
quests.reward: A Pony Point
quests.oneLine: Free Piaffe by clearing all the wooden crates burying his stable counter.
quests.how: Destroy every wooden crate around Piaffe; the fastest way is to set one on fire (a Fire Fruit, flame weapon, or torch) and let the flames spread to the rest. Once they're all gone you earn a Pony Point.

quests.name: A Picture for the Closed Stable I
quests.giver: Piaffe at Gerudo Canyon Stable, after Piaffe, Packed Away
quests.location: Gerudo Canyon Stable (photo taken from the East Gerudo Sky Archipelago)
quests.reward: A Pony Point and Chilly Mushroom Risotto
quests.oneLine: Take an aerial photo of Spectacle Rock to fill the first of Piaffe's empty frames.
quests.how: Examine the empty frames to start it, then launch from the Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower and use a rocket or fanplane to reach the East Gerudo Sky Archipelago; photograph the 'smiling' Spectacle Rock from above and show the picture to Piaffe.

quests.name: A Picture for the Closed Stable II
quests.giver: Piaffe at Gerudo Canyon Stable, after A Picture for the Closed Stable I
quests.location: Gerudo Canyon Stable (photo taken in the Gerudo Highlands)
quests.reward: A Pony Point and Spicy Mushroom Risotto
quests.oneLine: Photograph the giant sword in the Gerudo Highlands to fill Piaffe's second frame.
quests.how: Launch from the Gerudo Highlands Skyview Tower and glide to the giant sword landmark near Gerudo Summit (watch for the Frost Gleeok); photograph it and show the picture to Piaffe.

quests.name: Infiltrating the Yiga Clan
quests.giver: Mimos, outside the Yiga Clan Hideout in Karusa Valley (Gerudo Highlands, north-northwest of Gerudo Town)
quests.location: Yiga Clan Hideout, Karusa Valley (Gerudo Highlands)
quests.reward: Access to the Yiga Clan Hideout
quests.oneLine: Disguise yourself as a Yiga member to be let into their hideout.
quests.how: Collect and equip the full Yiga Armor set (head, top, and tights — found on Yiga members and in their stations), then approach and interact with the hideout's gate; the guard lets you in once you're wearing the complete disguise.

quests.name: The Yiga Clan Exam
quests.giver: The Yiga Blademaster at the Yiga Blademaster Station, after Infiltrating the Yiga Clan
quests.location: Yiga Blademaster Station, Gerudo region (Gerudo Canyon / behind a waterfall)
quests.reward: A Ruby and a Pristine Eightfold Longblade (also opens access to Suariwak Shrine)
quests.oneLine: Pass the Yiga's initiation by leaving banana offerings at their hidden frog statues.
quests.how: Wear the Yiga disguise, talk to the Blademaster to get five bunches of Mighty Bananas and the five frog-statue locations pinned on your map, then visit each frog statue (marked with the upside-down Yiga eye) and place a Mighty Banana on its offering plate. Return to the Blademaster to claim the Ruby and Pristine Eightfold Longblade.

quests.name: Ruffian-Infested Village
quests.giver: Rozel, hiding on the bluff at the entrance overlooking Lurelin Village (near Sifumim Shrine)
quests.reward: Frees the village and unlocks the Lurelin Village Restoration Project (Rozel has nothing to give until the town is rebuilt)
quests.oneLine: Monster-force pirates have seized Lurelin Village; clear every enemy occupying it to retake the town.
quests.how: Talk to Rozel to start, then sweep the whole village, beach, pirate ship and well, killing all the invaders (Bokoblins, Lizalfos, Moblins and a Boss Bokoblin on the ship). Fuse a strong horn or rock to a sturdy weapon for damage; since these enemies can't swim, you can knock the big ones into the sea to drown them. Clear every enemy marker to finish.

quests.name: Lurelin Village Restoration Project
quests.giver: Bolson, with Hudson's crew in the plaza of the cleared Lurelin Village
quests.reward: Rebuilds the village; the inn (free bed), restaurant (one free meal a day) and shops reopen, plus a free play at the Lucky Treasure Shop minigame
quests.oneLine: After the pirates are gone, help Bolson's crew rebuild Lurelin's wrecked buildings.
quests.how: Available after Ruffian-Infested Village; talk to Bolson, who needs 15 Logs and 20 Hylian Rice. Knock down regular trees (not the village palms) for logs and Ultrahand them to him, and cut grass around Faron for Hylian Rice, then keep talking to Bolson as each of the five buildings is restored until the project completes.

quests.name: Lurelin Resort Project
quests.giver: Bolson, in the rebuilt Lurelin Village
quests.reward: Unlocks the Lurelin Water Rally (a free water-racing attraction)
quests.oneLine: Help Bolson dream up a new tourist attraction for the rebuilt village — a water rally — and recruit racers for it.
quests.how: After the Restoration Project, talk to Bolson and suggest hosting a Water Rally. Travel to the Hudson Construction Site near Tarrey Town and recruit the two Gerudo racers there (fix their boat with Ultrahand, then beat their course), then return to Bolson in Lurelin to open the Water Rally.

quests.name: A Way to Trade, Washed Away
quests.giver: Garini, by the general store in Lurelin Village
quests.location: Lurelin Village / Tenoko Island, off the southern coast
quests.reward: Star Fragment; reopens Garini's General Store (free stock)
quests.oneLine: Garini's trading boat drifted out to sea in the pirate attack — retrieve a boat from Tenoko Island.
quests.how: Talk to Garini, then head out to Tenoko Island where a boat has washed ashore. Ultrahand a Zonai steering stick plus a fan and battery onto the stranded boat, sail it back to the pier at Lurelin, and report to Garini.

quests.name: Rattled Ralera
quests.giver: Ralera, in the rebuilt Lurelin Village
quests.reward: Lurelin Village Fabric (paraglider fabric; redeem at Sayge's dye shop in Hateno)
quests.oneLine: An anxious villager wants a spicy seafood dish to inspire a new village flag.
quests.how: Available after the village is restored; cook a Seafood Curry from a Porgy (e.g. Mighty/Armored Porgy), Hylian Rice and Goron Spice in a pot, then give it to Ralera. She'll be overwhelmed by the spice, so cool her down with a gust of wind (a Korok frond fused to a weapon, a Zonai fan, or Tulin's gust), which inspires her flag and earns the Lurelin Village Fabric.

quests.name: Dad's Blue Shirt
quests.giver: Zuta, on the beach in Lurelin Village
quests.reward: Island Lobster Shirt (light armor with Level 1 Heat Resistance)
quests.oneLine: A boy wants his dad's blue shirt back from an iron chest sunk in the bay.
quests.how: Available after rebuilding Lurelin; talk to Zuta, who points to an iron chest sunk in the shallows of the bay. Float a raft out (Ultrahand a plank onto a couple of logs, or use any floating platform) and use Ultrahand to drag the submerged iron chest up out of the water, then open it for the Island Lobster Shirt. Report back to Zuta to finish (you keep the shirt).

quests.name: Ousting the Giants
quests.giver: Kampo, at Lakeside Stable
quests.location: Lakeside Stable, southwestern Faron (near Lake Floria)
quests.reward: Mighty Fried Bananas; the three cleared caves become safe to mine
quests.oneLine: Three Hinox brothers are terrorizing the lakes around Lakeside Stable — hunt down all three.
quests.how: Talk to Kampo at Lakeside Stable to place three map pins, then clear each Hinox in its cave: a Blue Hinox in Corta Lake Cave, a regular Hinox in Rodai Lakefront Tunnel, and a Black Hinox in Calora Lake Cave. Shoot each one's eye to stagger it and pile on melee damage while it's down, then return to Kampo for the reward.

quests.name: Bring Peace to Faron
quests.giver: Flaxel (Monster Control Crew), beside the beached pirate ship down the Menoat River
quests.reward: Silver Rupee (100 rupees); unlocks the Bring Peace to Hebra side adventure
quests.oneLine: Help a Monster Control Crew squad storm a beached pirate ship after their bridge collapses.
quests.how: Talk to Flaxel by the wrecked bridge, then use Ultrahand to grab the fallen bridge section and rejoin its two halves so the crew can cross. Board the pirate ship and defeat the monsters on deck, then report back to Flaxel for the Silver Rupee.

quests.name: Goddess Statue of Courage
quests.giver: The Goddess Statue at the Spring of Courage (its request is voiced via the toppled statue behind Mayausiy Shrine at the Forgotten Temple)
quests.reward: Topaz
quests.oneLine: Pray at the Spring of Courage statue, which asks for a piece of the dragon Farosh.
quests.how: Pray at the Goddess Statue at the Spring of Courage to begin; the toppled statue at the Forgotten Temple asks for one of Farosh's Claws. Find the electric dragon Farosh as it flies over the Faron area (it commonly rises near the Hills of Baumer Chasm around 1 PM), shoot one of its front feet to drop a Farosh's Claw, then drop the claw into the spring water in front of the statue for a Topaz.

quests.name: The Flute Player's Plan
quests.giver: Pyper, up a tree just north of Highland Stable
quests.reward: Big Hearty Truffle
quests.oneLine: A young flute player wants sunset fireflies to make a glowing tree and reunite the Stable Trotters band.
quests.how: Talk to Pyper in the tree north of Highland Stable; he asks for 10 Sunset Fireflies. Catch them at night in the grass nearby (sneak up slowly or wear stealth gear) or buy them from Beedle, then give the 10 to Pyper. Afterward find the bandmate Haite at the stable at night to finish and receive a Big Hearty Truffle.

quests.name: A Picture for Lakeside Stable
quests.giver: Anly, by the empty picture frame inside Lakeside Stable
quests.location: Lakeside Stable, southwestern Faron (near Lake Floria)
quests.reward: Tough Tomato Seafood Soup + 1 Pony Point
quests.oneLine: The stable wants a photo of Floria Falls on a clear day to fill an empty frame.
quests.how: Talk to Anly to start (you need the camera from the Purah Pad / Hyrule Compendium). Wait for clear weather, travel up to Floria Falls, frame the waterfall and snap a photo, then return to Anly and show it from the photo album for a Tough Tomato Seafood Soup and a Pony Point.

quests.name: A Picture for Highland Stable
quests.giver: Padok, by the empty picture frame inside Highland Stable
quests.reward: Energizing Honey Crepe + 1 Pony Point
quests.oneLine: The stable wants proof the rumored Giant White Stallion is real — a photo of it.
quests.how: Talk to Padok to start (camera required). Ride south to find the Giant White Stallion roaming near Ibara Butte (south of Horse God Bridge); you don't need to tame it — just get close enough to photograph it, then return to Padok and show the photo for an Energizing Honey Crepe and a Pony Point.

quests.name: Legend of the Great Sky Island
quests.giver: Steward Construct, on the roof of the Temple of Time at the top of the Great Sky Island
quests.reward: Zonai Fabric (a paraglider fabric you apply at Kochi's Dye Shop in Hateno Village)
quests.oneLine: A timed challenge to light three large bonfires across the Great Sky Island and get back to the Temple of Time roof before time runs out, all without ever touching the ground.
quests.how: You must have completed the Restoring the Zora Armor side adventure first, since the Zora Armor is required. Talk to the Steward Construct on the Temple of Time roof to set the time to 7am, raise the waterfall islands, and start the roughly 12-in-game-hour timer; then glide between the three fire pits, riding the Zora Armor's waterfall-swim to refill stamina so you can stay airborne. Light each pit with any fire source (a fire arrow, Fire Fruit, or a Fused flame weapon) — falling into water is fine, but touching solid ground fails the run — then return to the Steward Construct before time expires.

quests.name: Messages from an Ancient Era
quests.giver: Wortsworth (first found at Lookout Landing examining a fallen stone tablet, then directs you to meet him at the Zonai Survey Team base in Kakariko Village); appears after some early main-quest progress
quests.location: Sky islands across all of Hyrule (giver is at Lookout Landing, then Kakariko Village, on the Surface)
quests.reward: Zonai Survey Team Fabric, plus 100 rupees per tablet (1,200 rupees total)
quests.oneLine: Help the archaeologist Wortsworth decipher ancient Zonai writing by photographing 12 engraved stone tablets perched on small flower-shaped sky islands floating high above Hyrule.
quests.how: Speak to Wortsworth at Lookout Landing, then meet him again at the Zonai Survey Team base in Kakariko Village to take the task. Reveal as much of the sky map as you can, then fly up to each of the 12 flower-shaped floating islands (a Zonai hover-bike or wing makes the long sky crossings easier; bring stamina food) and use the Purah Pad camera to photograph each tablet. The tablets can be done in any order — once all 12 are photographed, return to Wortsworth in Kakariko Village to turn them in.

quests.name: Ancient Blades Below
quests.giver: A Mining Construct standing in the Spirit Temple (the giant statue temple in the Faron Grasslands Depths)
quests.location: Spirit Temple, Faron Grasslands Depths (The Depths)
quests.reward: An Ancient Blade (a Fuse material that turns an arrow into an Ancient Arrow); repeatable, one Ancient Blade per 50 Zonaite
quests.oneLine: A Mining Construct deep in the Spirit Temple offers to forge you Ancient Blades in exchange for raw Zonaite.
quests.how: Finish the 'Guidance from Ages Past' main quest first, then talk to the Mining Construct in the Spirit Temple. Hand it 50 Zonaite (mine Zonaite ore deposits and the abandoned mines, or fight Depths enemies for it) to receive an Ancient Blade, and you can repeat the trade for another blade every 50 Zonaite.

quests.name: A Call from the Depths
quests.giver: A voice speaking through the Goddess Statue at the Temple of Time Ruins on the Great Plateau (reached after you destroy the boulders plugging a leak south of Hyrule Field Skyview Tower); it sends you to the eyeless Bargainer Statue in the Great Abandoned Central Mine in The Depths
quests.location: Bargainer Statue in the Great Abandoned Central Mine (The Depths); the four Eye Stones are gathered from chasms around the Great Plateau
quests.reward: Your choice of a Heart Container or Stamina Vessel, and it opens Poe trading at that Bargainer Statue (Poes buy the Tunic of the Depths and other gloom-resistant gear)
quests.oneLine: A statue robbed of its four eyes asks you to recover its missing Eye Stones from the chasms above and restore them on its body in the Depths.
quests.how: Collect four Eye Stones from chasms around the Great Plateau, freeing each one (melt the ice, smash the rock, or Ultrahand the stone out of the pond), then drop each into its nearby chasm and descend after it. Use Ultrahand to build a cart or flying rig on the Depths rails to ferry the Eye Stones to the Bargainer Statue in the Great Abandoned Central Mine, lighting the way with Lightroots, then place all four eyes to finish it and claim your vessel.

quests.name: Master Kohga of the Yiga Clan
quests.giver: Begins automatically after you defeat Master Kohga in the Great Abandoned Central Mine during the 'A Mystery in the Depths' main quest; you then chase him through the other abandoned mines
quests.location: The Depths — Abandoned Gerudo Mine, Abandoned Lanayru Mine, and Abandoned Hebra Mine
quests.reward: A Huge Crystallized Charge at each mine (and a Diamond at the last) plus three Zonai Schema Stones from the Steward Constructs: Hovercraft, Bolt Boat, and Rocket Platform
quests.oneLine: Hunt the disgraced Yiga leader Master Kohga across the Depths' abandoned mines as he pilots ever-bigger Zonai war machines.
quests.how: After the main-quest fight, follow Kohga's trail of Yiga statues to the Abandoned Gerudo Mine, then the Abandoned Lanayru Mine, then the Abandoned Hebra Mine, fighting him in a tougher Zonai contraption each time. Use Recall to fling his own bombs and rocks back at him, or Ultrahand-fuse a heavy boulder to a weapon to smash his machine; after each win grab the loot and the Hovercraft, Bolt Boat, and Rocket Platform schema stones from the nearby Steward Construct. Beating him at the Abandoned Hebra Mine ends the adventure.
